info.title: Customer Management API
info.description: APIs for the creation and management of customer information

post.description:
Creates a new **Billing Group** within the specified Customer.
A billing group defines how billing, invoicing, charges, and fees are
organised for one or more Legal Entities.

## Purpose
Billing Groups act as logical containers that determine:
- How billing rules are applied
- Which Legal Entities/Products participate in a given billing arrangement
- How charges and usage are aggregated
- How invoices are grouped

They provide a structural layer between Legal Entities and billing logic,
enabling more complex arrangements such as multi-entity billing,
consolidated invoicing, or separate billing streams within the same customer.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Newly created Billing Groups start with:
- `draft` version = `0`
- `submitted` version = `0`
- `approved` version = `0`

The provided payload populates the **draft** slot.

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft data is also copied to the submitted slot.
- The Billing Group is immediately **submitted for evaluation**.

If `submit` is omitted or false:
- The caller may submit the Billing Group later via the dedicated submit
  endpoint.

Evaluation may return:
- Missing configuration
- Inconsistent or invalid billing relationships
- Document or data requirements

Once all findings are resolved and re-evaluated, the approved version is
updated and used across billing processes.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Requires a valid JWT with the `CREATE_BILLING_GROUP` permission.
- The referenced customer must exist.
- The request body is validated; errors are returned in the response.

## Result
Returns the persisted Billing Group as an `EntityResponse` with a `201 Created`
status code.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Billing Groups** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing billing arrangements before selecting a specific billing
group for editing, submission, or association to legal entities/products.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided `customer_id`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned. If not provided, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data. Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Billing Group** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single billing
group prior to updates or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Returns 404 when the billing group is not found or not visible to caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<BillingGroup>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Billing Group

put.description:
Updates an existing **Billing Group** draft.

## Purpose
Refine billing configuration before submission or after evaluation feedback
(e.g., invoice grouping, charging rules, entity participation).

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies changes to the submitted slot and triggers
  evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the billing group should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Billing Group** within the specified customer.

## Purpose
Remove erroneous or obsolete billing arrangements prior to activation or
usage.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation is scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Billing Group

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Billing Group** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Validate billing configuration (associations, charge rules, invoicing logic)
before it is applied for live billing.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged
  until evaluation/apply completes.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the billing group for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Billing Group**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external changes) even
without new draft submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_BILLING_GROUP`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Contacts** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing contacts before selecting one for update, submission, or
association to legal entities and workflows.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_CONTACT`.
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes results to the provided `customer_id`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned. If not provided, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data. Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

post.description:
Creates a new **Contact** for the specified Customer.
A contact represents an individual associated with the customer or its
underlying legal entities, typically used for communication, operational
coordination, and compliance-related processes.

## Purpose
Contacts provide a structured, versioned record of:
- Key individuals linked to the customer
- Their roles, responsibilities, and relationship to the business
- Points of communication for operational, billing, or compliance workflows

Contacts are an important supporting entity, enabling downstream processes
such as onboarding, risk evaluation, customer servicing, and regulatory
checks.

## Lifecycle & Versions
Newly created contacts follow the standard entity lifecycle:
- **draft**, **submitted**, and **approved** version numbers all begin at `0`
- The draft copy is populated with the data provided in the request

If `submit=true` is provided:
- The draft copy is also written to the submitted slot
- The contact is immediately **submitted for evaluation**

If omitted:
- The contact remains in draft form until explicitly submitted
  via the submit endpoint

The **approved** slot is updated only once evaluation completes and the apply
workflow has run successfully.

## Submission & Evaluation
During evaluation the system may identify:
- Missing or invalid personal details
- Required supporting information
- Policy or compliance checks triggered by the data

Any findings are returned to the caller and must be resolved before the
contact can be approved.

## Validation & Authorisation
- Caller must have `CREATE_CONTACT` permission for the target customer.
- A valid JWT from the authorisation server must accompany the request.
- Payload validation errors are returned with structured details.

## Result
Returns the newly created Contact wrapped in `EntityResponse` with a
**201 Created** response.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the contact should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Contact** by ID.

## Purpose
Display the current draft/submitted/approved state for a single contact
before editing or submission.

## Behaviour
- Requires `READ_CONTACT`.
- Returns 404 when the contact is not found or not visible to caller.
- Includes version/status context in `EntityResponse<Contact>`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Contact

put.description:
Updates an existing **Contact** draft.

## Purpose
Refine contact details (roles, communication info, responsibilities) before
submission or after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_CONTACT`.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `EntityUpdateRequest.version`.
- Optional `submit` flag copies the change to the submitted slot and
  triggers evaluation.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the contact change should be immediately submitted

requestBody.description: Versioned update of the Contact details.

delete.description:
Deletes an existing **Contact** within the specified customer.

## Purpose
Remove erroneous or obsolete contact records prior to activation or use in
workflows.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_CONTACT`.
- Returns 200 on successful deletion; 404 if the record does not exist.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the Contact

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Contact** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Validate contact information and associated compliance checks before making
the contact available to downstream processes.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_CONTACT`.
- Copies the draft data to the submitted slot; approved data is unchanged
  until evaluation/apply completes.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.
- Authorisation scoped to the customer.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the contact for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Contact**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external data changes)
without submitting new draft content.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_CONTACT`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the entity for the operation

get.description:
Returns the paginated collection of **Legal Entities** for a customer.

## Purpose
Discover existing legal entities, typically to show summaries in dashboards
or to locate a specific entity before drilling into details.

## Behaviour
- Paginates via `PaginationSettings`.
- Scopes to the supplied `customer_id`; caller must have `READ_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns lightweight `EntityResponse<LegalEntity>` entries.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of items to be returned. If not provided, 0 is assumed.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of items in a page that will be returned.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The fields by which the returned data will be sorted. Specified as a csv list of fields. See endpoint description for details of supported fields for sorting.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: The sort order for the returned data. Can be either ASC for ascending or DESC for descending.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Cursor for pagination; typically encoded as a string understood by the endpoint.

200.description: Successfully returned data. Data is returned in the body of the response as an array of json objects up to the maximum specified in the pagination settings.

post.description:
Creates a new **Legal Entity**, the core business object representing an organisation, its ownership, operational details, and beneficial parties.

## Purpose
A Legal Entity is the foundation of the merchant lifecycle.
It provides the authoritative record of:
- Business identity and structure.
- Ownership and persons of significant control.
- Operational characteristics.
- Relationships to other legal entities (for complex hierarchies).

## Typical Use Cases
- Onboarding a new merchant or partner.
- Registering a new business entity within an existing group.
- Defining hierarchical corporate structures.

## Submission & Evaluation
After creation, the Legal Entity may be **submitted for approval**.
Submission triggers the evaluation workflow, which may identify:
- Missing or inconsistent business information.
- Required supporting documents.
- Compliance or risk-related questions.

Any findings are returned to the client. Once addressed and re-evaluated,
the Legal Entity can be **applied**, making its data active across all
downstream systems.

## Related Domains
Legal Entities link to several other resource domains:
- Products.
- Trading locations.
- Bank accounts.

These are managed by their respective APIs but form part of the overall
entity lifecycle.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the legal entity should be immediately submitted

get.description:
Retrieves a specific **Legal Entity** by its ID.

## Purpose
Fetch the current representation of a single legal entity to display or
validate data prior to updates/submission.

## Behaviour
- Enforces customer scoping and `READ_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns 404 when the entity does not exist or is not visible to the caller.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

put.description:
Updates an existing **Legal Entity** draft (business data).

## Purpose
Apply changes to core legal-entity attributes before submission or as part
of remediation after evaluation feedback.

## Behaviour
- Requires `UPDATE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Supports optional immediate submission via `submit` query flag.
- Uses optimistic concurrency via `version` inside `EntityUpdateRequest`.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the item to be updated

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: flag to indicate if the legal entity change should be immediately submitted

delete.description:
Soft-deletes a **Legal Entity** (logical delete).

## Purpose
Retire an entity that was created in error or is no longer needed prior to
activation.

## Behaviour
- Requires `DELETE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Returns 200 on success, 404 if the entity cannot be found.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity

post.description:
Submits the current draft of a **Legal Entity** for evaluation.

## Purpose
Trigger the compliance/risk evaluation workflow that validates completeness,
required documents, and business rules.

## Behaviour
- Requires `SUBMIT_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- On acceptance, copies draft to submitted slot and starts evaluation.
- Returns 202 Accepted with submission metadata.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for the operation

requestBody.description: Details about the draft that should be submitted for approval.

post.description:
Triggers re-evaluation for the specified **Legal Entity**.

## Purpose
Manually start the evaluation workflow (e.g., after external data changes)
without submitting new draft content.

## Behaviour
- Requires `EVALUATE_LEGAL_ENTITY`.
- Invokes service-level evaluation for the customer context.
- Returns 200 once the evaluation request is queued/processed.

parameters.name: customer_id
parameters.description: Id of the customer for the operation

parameters.name: entity_id
parameters.description: Id of the legal entity for the operation
